summary: Qualify a lead or opportunity using structured frameworks to produce a scored recommendation and next-step plan.

inputs.name: lead_data
inputs.description: Lead or opportunity details including company, contact, and engagement history.

inputs.name: qualification_framework
inputs.description: Framework to apply: MEDDIC, BANT, or CHAMP.

inputs.name: icp_definition
inputs.description: Ideal customer profile criteria for fit assessment.

outputs.name: qualification_score
outputs.description: Scored assessment across framework dimensions with evidence.

outputs.name: fit_assessment
outputs.description: ICP fit analysis with gap identification.

outputs.name: next_step_plan
outputs.description: Recommended actions based on qualification outcome.

budget.notes: Focus on evidence-based scoring and clear next steps.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is lead or opportunity qualification, not marketing lead scoring.
Require lead data and framework selection before scoring.
Return qualification score, fit assessment, and next steps in one consistent output.

steps.action: Collect and validate lead data against ICP criteria.
Score the opportunity using the selected qualification framework.
Evaluate ICP fit and identify gaps.
Produce next-step recommendations based on qualification outcome.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read lead data, CRM exports, and ICP definitions.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Save qualification assessment when artifact is requested.

verification.method: Check that all framework dimensions are scored with evidence.
Check that next steps have owners and timelines.
Check that disqualification criteria were explicitly evaluated.

success_criteria: The qualification score distinguishes evidence from assumptions.
Next steps are specific enough for immediate seller follow-up.

rollback.when: If the qualification invents lead facts or misapplies the framework, roll back to raw lead data and rerun.
